summary: 0 ERRORs require a code-fix script. 7 unaddressed ERRORs split into three buckets: (1) deliberate NotImplementedError stubs awaiting upstream substrate / cascade-gate landings -- V3-EXQ-449c, V3-EXQ-455a (carryover from earlier staging reports; no fix possible until V3-EXQ-476 cascade-gate retest passes); (2) false ERRORs where a valid PASS manifest exists on disk but runner mis-classified -- V3-EXQ-244a (SIGTERM mid-run on cloud-2 but a follow-on production run completed with elapsed_seconds=19395s ~5.4h and PASS, manifest committed 2026-05-07), V3-EXQ-542 (ARC-062 Phase 1 substrate-readiness diagnostic, runner saw no sentinel print but manifest is PASS on disk; CLAUDE.md confirms 5/5 PASS), V3-EXQ-544 (MECH-313 noise-floor substrate-readiness, same sentinel-parse gap; CLAUDE.md confirms 5/5 PASS); (3) infrastructure failures (cloud SIGTERM, no manifest) -- V3-EXQ-495 (MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate, ~4h on cloud-1 then -15), V3-EXQ-538 (SD-049 Phase 2 reef behavioural, ~24min on cloud-1 then -15, same cluster as V3-EXQ-433f / V3-EXQ-537 silent-fail). Two queue entries proposed for human confirmation: V3-EXQ-495a and V3-EXQ-538a (Mac-pinned reruns, no code change). One governance handoff: rebuild_indexes + mark_discussed for 244a/542/544.

diagnoses.root_cause: Script raises NotImplementedError on purpose: 'V3-EXQ-449c gated. MECH-284 Phase 3 substrate landed 2026-04-24 but cascade gate V3-EXQ-476a/476b ran FAIL (V_s wired-but-inert -- catatonic-lock at policy layer). Remaining blockers: (a) SD-037 (broadcast override regulator, orexin-analog) -- next /implement-substrate target per substrate_queue.json -- to break the monostrategy lock; (b) MECH-074b hippocampal consumer wiring landed on SD-035.' Exit code 1, ~0.1s -- the stub correctly refused to run.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. Not a code bug. SD-037 substrate DID land 2026-04-25 (CLAUDE.md confirms). What remains: (i) the V_s cascade-gate retest after SD-037 (and the subsequent staleness-into-gate wiring of 2026-04-29) -- V3-EXQ-476 itself; (ii) MECH-074b retrieval-bias-aware replay path on SD-035. Both are outside the diagnose-errors workflow.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: No experiment to re-tag. When the upstream blockers clear, this should be re-queued as a NEW EXQ via /queue-experiment with re-evaluated claim_ids -- not a letter suffix on 449c.
diagnoses.next_step_for_user: Add V3-EXQ-449c to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs (already flagged in prior staging reports; carryover). When V3-EXQ-476 cascade gate PASSes, /queue-experiment a fresh ID for MECH-074b validation.

diagnoses.root_cause: Script raises NotImplementedError on purpose: 'V3-EXQ-455a gated. MECH-284 Phase 3 substrate landed 2026-04-24, but cascade gate V3-EXQ-476a/476b ran FAIL (V_s wired-but-inert: anchor resets fire but action selection unchanged -- catatonic-lock at policy layer). Remaining blocker is SD-037 (broadcast override regulator, orexin-analog).' Exit code 1, ~0.05s -- stub correctly refused to run.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. SD-037 substrate DID land 2026-04-25 (CLAUDE.md confirms). The remaining blocker is the V_s cascade-gate retest (V3-EXQ-476-equivalent successor) confirming V_s flags break baseline monostrategy lock. Outside diagnose-errors scope.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: No experiment to re-tag. Re-queue as NEW EXQ when the V_s cascade-gate retest PASSes.
diagnoses.next_step_for_user: Add V3-EXQ-455a to review_tracker.discussed_experiment_dirs (carryover from prior reports). Re-queue as a fresh EXQ once V_s cascade-gate retest passes.

diagnoses.root_cause: runner_status.json reports ERROR (exit -15 on ree-cloud-2 at 2026-05-06T09:28:53Z after 80min) but a real PASS manifest exists on disk at REE_assembly/evidence/experiments/v3_exq_244a_mech165_replay_diversity_validation_v3.json with elapsed_seconds=19395 (~5.4h) and outcome=PASS, evidence_direction=supports, claim_ids=['MECH-165']. Manifest committed 2026-05-07T05:27Z (the day after the runner-reported SIGTERM). A follow-on production run completed normally after the SIGTERM but runner_status was never updated. NOTE: the 2026-05-06 staging report initially flagged this manifest as a smoke-test artifact -- that was correct at the time of writing (elapsed was implausibly small), but the file was subsequently overwritten by a real production run.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None. Manifest is on disk; governance can pick it up.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Tags inherit from the manifest already on disk. No re-tag required.
diagnoses.next_step_for_user: Run /governance to ingest the on-disk manifest into claim_evidence.v1.json and mark V3-EXQ-244a discussed in review_tracker.json. No rerun needed.

diagnoses.root_cause: MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate (VTA / hippocampally-planned arm) ran for 14383s (~4h) on ree-cloud-1 starting 2026-04-28T17:14Z, then exit -15 (SIGTERM) at 21:13Z. estimated_minutes was 1500 (~25h) -- ran a quarter of expected duration before the worker killed the process. Hetzner CX22 (cloud-1) is undersized per memory reference reference_cloud_workers.md; cloud-1 throughput is ~0.23 min/ep at 200 steps/ep vs ~0.10 min/ep on Mac, so this experiment's full duration was estimated at ~40h on cloud-1 vs ~17h on Mac. The 4h SIGTERM is most consistent with a Hetzner platform-side restart/limit rather than a code path. No manifest on disk.
diagnoses.fix_applied: No code change required. Successor V3-EXQ-495a re-queues the same script with machine_affinity pinned away from ree-cloud-1. Mac-pinned (DLAPTOP-4.local) per the calibration table in ree-v3/CLAUDE.md (Mac ~0.10 min/ep -> ~17h single-machine; defensive budget 1500min = 25h).
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Inherited from predecessor and re-evaluated. The script is unchanged in design (MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate, dual goal-directed systems test). Only the machine affinity changes; claim_ids remain MECH-163 unchanged.

draft_queue_entry.title: MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate -- VTA / hippocampally-planned arm (Mac-pinned rerun)
draft_queue_entry.note: Successor to V3-EXQ-495 which SIGTERMed on ree-cloud-1 after ~4h of an expected ~40h (cloud-1 calibrated at ~0.23 min/ep). No code change. Mac-pinned per Mac calibration ~17h single-machine. Same script, same design.

diagnoses.next_step_for_user: Confirm Mac-pinned rerun is the right call (vs. shrinking scope, splitting into smaller arms, or moving to Daniel-PC). Then append draft_queue_entry to ree-v3/experiment_queue.json and validate. NOTE: 17h on Mac is a substantial commitment -- worth confirming science value before queueing.

diagnoses.root_cause: SD-049 Phase 2 reef behavioural validation, sleep-on ablation, ran 1470s (~24min) on ree-cloud-1 starting 2026-05-08T12:29Z then SIGTERM at 12:54Z. estimated_minutes was 220. This is the same 2026-05-08 cloud-1 silent-fail cluster as V3-EXQ-433f and V3-EXQ-537, all of which produced no manifest and were silently dropped (see V3-EXQ-537a note in experiment_queue.json git history flagging the pattern explicitly). The 2026-05-09 manifest-leak fix in experiment_runner._git_push_with_retry addresses the conflict-recovery path that was destroying manifests, but did not necessarily resolve the underlying cloud-1 instability.
diagnoses.fix_applied: No code change. Successor V3-EXQ-538a re-queues with machine_affinity pinned to DLAPTOP-4.local, matching the V3-EXQ-537a precedent and the queue-note flag for that experiment.
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Re-evaluated: still the correct tag. The script tests SD-049 Phase 2 behavioural validation with sleep ablation paired to V3-EXQ-514f. Same design, same claim under test.

draft_queue_entry.title: SD-049 Phase 2 reef behavioural validation, sleep-on ablation (Mac-pinned rerun, paired to 514f)
draft_queue_entry.note: Successor to V3-EXQ-538 which silent-failed on ree-cloud-1 (same 2026-05-08 cluster as V3-EXQ-433f, V3-EXQ-537). Mac-pinned per V3-EXQ-537a precedent. No code change. The 2026-05-09 manifest-leak fix may have addressed the conflict-recovery destruction path, but pin to Mac to be safe.

diagnoses.next_step_for_user: Confirm Mac-pinned rerun. Append draft_queue_entry to ree-v3/experiment_queue.json and validate.

diagnoses.root_cause: Runner reported ERROR ('No runner sentinel emitted and no PASS/FAIL on stdout; exit=0; secs=2.2') on 2026-05-09T20:22:11Z on Mac. But a real PASS manifest exists on disk: REE_assembly/evidence/experiments/v3_exq_542_arc062_gated_policy_substrate_readiness_v3_20260509T202211Z.json with result=PASS, evidence_direction=supports, claim_ids including ARC-062. The script ran cleanly and wrote PASS to the canonical manifest, but did not print the runner-expected 'verdict:' / 'PASS' sentinel line to stdout. CLAUDE.md ARC-062 Phase 1 entry explicitly confirms 'V3-EXQ-542 5/5 PASS 2026-05-09T20:22:11Z'.
diagnoses.fix_applied: None at the experiment level. Script could be patched to add a sentinel print so future smoke runs are correctly classified by the runner, but no rerun is needed (manifest is on disk).
diagnoses.claim_ids_rationale: Inherited from the manifest already on disk. No re-tag required.
diagnoses.next_step_for_user: (a) Run /governance to ingest the manifest into claim_evidence.v1.json (already in pending_review.md unclaimed-manifests table). (b) Mark V3-EXQ-542 discussed in review_tracker.json. (c) Optional follow-on: patch the substrate-readiness-diagnostic script template to emit a sentinel line so the runner correctly classifies future smoke runs.

Stop these from re-appearing as undiagnosed ERRORs in future diagnose-errors runs

infrastructure_observation.recurring_pattern: Hetzner CX22 cloud workers (ree-cloud-1, ree-cloud-2) periodically SIGTERM long-running experiments or silently drop manifests. Confirmed incidents: 2026-04-28 V3-EXQ-495 (4h cloud-1 SIGTERM); 2026-05-06 V3-EXQ-244a / V3-EXQ-530 (coincident cloud-1+cloud-2 SIGTERM); 2026-05-08 V3-EXQ-433f / V3-EXQ-537 / V3-EXQ-538 silent-fail cluster on cloud-1.
infrastructure_observation.partial_mitigation_2026-05-09: experiment_runner._git_push_with_retry manifest-leak in conflict-recovery FIXED 2026-05-09 (captures pre-reset HEAD SHA; restores result_files via git checkout from pre-reset SHA after reset+stash-pop; contract test C1/C2/C3 in tests/contracts/test_runner_manifest_survives_conflict_recovery.py). This addresses the specific path that destroyed manifests during conflict-recovery push retries; does not necessarily address all cloud-1 capacity / push-instability issues.
infrastructure_observation.recommendation: For high-priority or long-duration experiments, prefer Mac-pinning over cloud-1 until the platform-side cause is understood. The cloud workers remain useful for short / lower-priority runs where a SIGTERM or manifest drop is recoverable. Consider whether a watchdog / re-queue mechanism in the runner could auto-retry SIGTERM runs (currently they are silently dropped from the queue per queue completion behaviour rules).
